searchDocs.title: Pair​
searchDocs.content: </> interface Pair { key: K value: V } A single key-value entry, as returned by OrderedDictionary:pairs.

searchDocs.title: ClassName​
searchDocs.content: Field </> OrderedDictionary.ClassName: "OrderedDictionary" A constant identifier for the class.

searchDocs.title: new​
searchDocs.content: </> OrderedDictionary.new() → OrderedDictionary<K,V> Creates a new, empty OrderedDictionary.

searchDocs.title: getLength​
searchDocs.content: </> OrderedDictionary:getLength() → number Returns the number of key-value pairs in the dictionary.

searchDocs.title: get​
searchDocs.content: </> OrderedDictionary:get(key: K) → V Returns the value associated with key, or nil if the key is not present.

searchDocs.title: set​
searchDocs.content: </> OrderedDictionary:set( key: K, value: V ) → () Sets the value for key. If the key is new, it is appended to the end of the order. If the key already exists, its value is replaced in place, keeping its position. If value is nil, the key is removed from the dictionary.

searchDocs.title: keyAtIndex​
searchDocs.content: </> OrderedDictionary:keyAtIndex(index: number) → ( K, V ) Returns the key and value at the given 1-based position in the order. Positive indices count from the front (1 is the first entry); negative indices count from the back (-1 is the last entry). Throws if index is zero or its magnitude exceeds the length.

searchDocs.title: indexAtKey​
searchDocs.content: </> OrderedDictionary:indexAtKey(key: K) → number? Returns the 1-based position of key in the order, or nil if the key is not present.

searchDocs.title: iterate​
searchDocs.content: </> OrderedDictionary:iterate(reversed: boolean?) → () → ( K?, V?, number? ) Returns an iterator over the entries in insertion order, suitable for use in a generic for loop. Pass true to iterate from last to first instead. Each step yields the key, the value, and the entry's 1-based position in the order (the same value OrderedDictionary:indexAtKey would return for that key, regardless of direction). for key, value, index in dict:iterate() do print(index, key, value) end

searchDocs.title: swap​
searchDocs.content: </> OrderedDictionary:swap( keyA: K, keyB: K ) → () Swaps the positions of keyA and keyB in the order. Each key keeps its own value; only their positions are exchanged. Throws if either key is not present.

searchDocs.title: clear​
searchDocs.content: </> OrderedDictionary:clear() → () Removes every entry, resetting the dictionary to empty.

searchDocs.title: keys​
searchDocs.content: </> OrderedDictionary:keys() → {K} Returns an array of the keys in insertion order.

searchDocs.title: values​
searchDocs.content: </> OrderedDictionary:values() → {V} Returns an array of the values in insertion order.

searchDocs.title: pairs​
searchDocs.content: </> OrderedDictionary:pairs() → { Pair<K, V> } Returns an array of Pair entries in insertion order.

searchDocs.title: sort​
searchDocs.content: </> OrderedDictionary:sort(compare: ( Pair<K, V>, Pair<K, V> ) → boolean) → () Reorders the entries in place using compare, which receives two Pair values and should return true when the first should come before the second (the same contract as table.sort). dict:sort(function(a, b) return a.value < b.value end)
